taxonomy: Scoria, a type of igneous rock, is classified as a type of volcanic glass. It is often formed when lava is ejected into the air and rapidly cools, resulting in a porous and lightweight texture. Due to its characteristics, Scoria is typically classified as a type of spatter or scoria, which is a subtype of volcanic rock. Scoria is often confused with other types of rocks, such as cinder or pumice, but it has distinct properties that set it apart. The classification of Scoria can vary depending on the specific location and composition of the rock.
